body.title: ToO §1.3/§2: add bus=network, shim=easy-interface framing
body.result: ToO §1.3 mental model callout + §2.1 shim sentence updated
body.intention: I intend that ToO §1.3 and §2 state the bus=network / shim=interface framing explicitly so any reader — human or CC — understands the conceptual model without re-explanation.
body.description:
The bus and shims together are the rack's internal network, but ToO currently describes them mechanically (IMAP, envelope fields, idle_wait) without ever stating the simple framing: 'the bus IS the network; shims are each device's interface to that network, making it easy for devices to talk without knowing transport details.'

Add a 'Bus + Shim mental model' callout at the top of §1.3 and a cross-reference sentence in §2.1 where the shim is first introduced.

**Affected files:** docs/TheoryOfOperation.md
**Design rules:** none apply (docs change only)
**Scope boundary:** Add framing paragraphs to §1.3 and §2.1 only. No code changes. Do not restructure existing sections.
**Completion criteria:** §1.3 opens with an explicit framing paragraph stating 'The bus is the rack's network; the shim is each device's interface to that network.' §2.1 references the shim's role as the device's network adapter in a single sentence. The existing mechanical detail (IMAP, envelope, IDLE loop) is unchanged.
